title: Remember Better: - Listen and Repeat: Say Key Points Aloud After Hearing Them
subtitle: Boost Recall with the Modality Effect
description:
To remember better:
- Listen and repeat: Say key points aloud after hearing them.
- Mix modalities: Write down spoken info or speak aloud written notes.
- Review audio: Record important notes and listen back later.

Example: After a meeting, record a summary of key points and listen to it during your commute.
keywords: Boost Recall with the Modality Effect

howTo.title: Record Immediate Summaries
howTo.summary: After meetings or calls, pause to identify up to three key items. Record a 30 to 90-second audio summary capturing decisions, owners, and next steps.

howTo.title: Tag and Store Clips
howTo.summary: Label each audio clip with a quick tag, such as MeetingName_YYYYMMDD. This helps in organizing and retrieving your summaries later.

howTo.title: Playback Within 24 Hours
howTo.summary: Listen to your recorded clips within 24 hours during routine activities like commuting. This reinforces memory and aids in recall.

howTo.title: Schedule Spaced Reviews
howTo.summary: Plan to re-listen to your clips on day 3 and day 10. This spaced repetition enhances retention and helps solidify the information.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and progress in the Brali LifeOS app to track your memory improvement journey. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/modality-effect-memory-booster/.
